64cef6195bf080dd9900d369.question: A resident of this location has a nightmare that a fire starts in this location, causing them to murder a man and using his blood to put out the fire. A resident of this location instructed their niece to use (+) pig’s blood to cleanse herself of the murder of Absyrtus. A man who died in this location asked to be cremated with only an (*) oar to mark his grave. According to a lost epic, a resident of this location made three figures immortal before marrying her former lover’s son. Her son killed that former lover with the spine of a stingray; that lover’s body was buried in this location. Elpenor fell off a ladder and broke his neck in this location. Eurylochus refused to enter a house in this location, which is guarded by docile wolves and lions. The men that did enter that house were turned into swine and had to be saved by Odysseus. For ten points, name this island, the home of a powerful sorceress and daughter of Helios.
64cef6195bf080dd9900d369.question_sanitized: A resident of this location has a nightmare that a fire starts in this location, causing them to murder a man and using his blood to put out the fire. A resident of this location instructed their niece to use (+) pig's blood to cleanse herself of the murder of Absyrtus. A man who died in this location asked to be cremated with only an (*) oar to mark his grave. According to a lost epic, a resident of this location made three figures immortal before marrying her former lover's son. Her son killed that former lover with the spine of a stingray; that lover's body was buried in this location. Elpenor fell off a ladder and broke his neck in this location. Eurylochus refused to enter a house in this location, which is guarded by docile wolves and lions. The men that did enter that house were turned into swine and had to be saved by Odysseus. For ten points, name this island, the home of a powerful sorceress and daughter of Helios.
64cef6195bf080dd9900d369.answer: Aeaea (or Aiaia, accept Circe’s island before island and any reasonable pronunciation (the actual pronunciation is ee-ee-uh), prompt afterwards)
